titleStmt.title: Letter from Sir Wilfrid Spender to his wife Lady Lillian Spender, 1 August 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Wilfrid Bliss Spender

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter Spender from sir Wilfrid Spender (1876-1960) to his wife, Lady Lillian Spender, née Dean, (1880-1968). In this letter, Spender writes that he is hungry in two ways, both for his dinner and for 'my rabbit pie' (his pet name for Lady Lillian being 'pink rabbit'). He gives details of a country excursion he made upon his horse Fanny, whose tendency towards distraction he compares to Lillian's propensity to 'swerve into hatshops' and 'loiter at the local Debenham's'. He concludes that Fanny is the better walking companion of the two. Spender also plans a holiday for Lillian. The letter is unfinished.Sir Wilfrid Spender was a member of the British Army until 1913 when controversy surrounding his signature of the Ulster Solemn League and Covenant resulted in his resignation. Spender was called up for military service in 1914 and from 1915 served in the 36th (Ulster) Division. Throughout the war, Spender wrote a collection of letters to his wife Lady Lillian Spender. The letters date mainly from 1915 to 1918, when Sir Wilfrid was serving in France. The letters concern politics, the Irish question, personal life, and Wilfrid's experience of the Great War. Lady Lillian served in the Ulster Volunteer Nursing Corps during the war, and was an organiser of the Ulster Division Comforts Fund.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Aug 1 My p r A rather hungry b w, hungry
in 2 ways principally for his dinner
of course as it is getting on for 9 & I
am writing in the office till relieved. It
is the quiet time of the day & if I
had more to say except that I am hungry
for â my rabbit pie. I could prattle on. Very hot today & I felt so hungry sleepy after
lunch that I struck and left the office
for a country ride & walk with Fan.
Fan is so good in walking beside me
but like another smaller animal
prances off when she sees chocolates â or
their equivalent â but she does not
swerve at hat shops, nor loiter at the
local Debenhams so I think on the
whole she is the better companion of the
2! She also keeps up with the b w on
the roads as well as on the grass, &
does not glance behind at taxies â
at least she wouldn't if there were any
taxies. Besides when the b w is lazy she
carries him which the p r never does whilst
the b w has never had to carry Fanny! I feel very much for the p r in this
scorching weather. I'm afraid she must be
fearfully limp & remember that a good
holiday when you can take it is not a
luxury but a necessity. Rex will of course
be delighted to see you settled at Bagley wood but
the sea side may be better but I don't
think less than one week is any use,
at the sea and probably 1 month 1
fortnight ought to be the minimum,
but I expect your family will be trotting
off soon or a hotel or rooms near
the other family (Edo?) might do I hope your work is getting less now.
